title: cichorium intybus var. sylvestre
intro: Cichorium intybus var. sylvestre, also known as wild chicory or blue dandelion, is a variety of the Cichorium intybus plant, a flowering plant in the dandelion tribe of the sunflower family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its use as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive aid, which can help to reduce swelling, improve digestion, and promote the removal of toxins from the body. The health benefits of wild chicory include its ability to support liver and kidney function, lower blood pressure, and provide antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Wild chicory can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used as a coffee substitute, while also being used in salads and as a vegetable in some cuisines.
